intro: Galarhoeus hakutosanensis, also known as Hakuto, is a type of plant in the family Urticaceae, native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, sore throats, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Galarhoeus hakutosanensis include triterpenoid saponins, such as galarhakutoside and galarhakutoisoside, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Galarhoeus hakutosanensis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever and cold-related symptoms.
